description: Test your knowledge of entertainment laser safety standards and E1.46 requirements

questions.explanation: ESTA (Entertainment Services and Technology Association) developed ANSI E1.46 for entertainment laser safety.

questions.text: What is the minimum vertical separation distance for supervised installations from the audience?
questions.explanation: E1.46 requires a minimum 3-meter vertical separation between laser beams and the audience for supervised installations.

questions.text: What is the required scan failure detection response time?
questions.explanation: Scan failure detection must respond within a few milliseconds to prevent hazardous static beam exposure.

questions.text: What MPE time base can be used for trained personnel in supervised installations?
questions.explanation: For trained personnel in supervised installations, the 0.25-second aversion response (blink reflex) MPE may be used.

questions.text: Software blanking alone is sufficient as a safety measure for audience scanning.
questions.explanation: E1.46 states that software blanking should not be used as the sole safety measure. Hardware protection is required.

questions.text: Which of the following are required for scan failure safety systems? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Scan failure systems must respond within milliseconds and fail-safe to beam-off. Automatic restart is prohibited.

questions.text: What is the minimum horizontal separation distance for supervised installations?
questions.explanation: E1.46 requires a minimum 2.5-meter horizontal separation from the audience for supervised installations.

questions.text: For unsupervised installations, what additional requirements apply?
options: Lower MPE limits and enhanced physical barriers
questions.explanation: Unsupervised installations require more conservative MPE limits and enhanced physical barriers since no operator is present.

questions.text: Which checks are required during pre-show verification? (Select all that apply)
options: MPE measurements verified for audience areas
questions.explanation: Pre-show verification must include emergency stop testing, scan failure system verification, and MPE measurement confirmation.

questions.text: What exposure time base should be used for audience members in intentional exposure scenarios?
questions.explanation: For audience members, the MPE should be based on at least 10 seconds of continuous exposure as the time base.

questions.text: When using diffraction effects (mirror balls), what must be considered?
options: Number of facets and power per reflected beam
Only the color of the laser
questions.explanation: The safety of mirror balls depends on the number of facets splitting the beam and the resulting power per reflected beam.

questions.text: Pulsed lasers (Q-switched) are acceptable for audience scanning with proper precautions.
questions.explanation: E1.46 states that pulsed lasers (including Q-switched) should not be used where audience exposure is intended or likely.
